2605.22923.title: AI-Friendly LaTeX: Using LaTeX Code as a Knowledge Source for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
2605.22923.paper_summary: Large language models can answer questions about textbooks, lecture notes, and programming exercises more reliably when their answers are grounded in an explicit knowledge source. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a common approach: relevant fragments of a document are retrieved and inserted into the model context before answering. For mathematical and technical material, the original LaTeX source can be a better starting point than a PDF, because it contains structural information, labels, sectioning commands, macros, and authorial intent that are often lost or distorted in PDF extraction. However, LaTeX source is not automatically AI-friendly. Cross-references must be resolved, custom macros must be interpreted, exercises and examples must be identified, and author-supplied semantic metadata may be needed. This article describes a focused preprocessing approach for turning LaTeX source, together with its compiled auxiliary files and optional author annotations, into Markdown and JSONL chunks suitable for indexing in a vector database.
2605.22923.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型在回答有關教科書、講義和程式練習的問題時，當其答案基於明確的知識來源時，能夠更可靠地提供回應。檢索增強生成（RAG）是一種常見的方法：相關的文檔片段會被檢索並插入到模型的上下文中，然後再進行回答。對於數學和技術材料而言，原始的 LaTeX 源碼可能比 PDF 更適合作為起點，因為它包含了結構信息、標籤、分段命令、宏和作者意圖，而這些在 PDF 提取中往往會丟失或扭曲。然而，LaTeX 源碼並不是自動對 AI 友好的。交叉引用必須被解決，自定義宏必須被解釋，練習和例子必須被識別，並且可能需要作者提供的語義元數據。本文描述了一種專注的預處理方法，用於將 LaTeX 源碼及其編譯的輔助文件和可選的作者註釋轉換為適合在向量數據庫中索引的 Markdown 和 JSONL 區塊。
2605.22923.author: Tom Verhoeff et.al.
